questions.prompt: Which hand opens most profitably from UTG in 9-max?

answers.explanation: Marked correct so this question still has exactly one is_correct; the deliberate fault here is only three answers.
Dominated by the ranges behind you UTG.
Too disconnected and dominated to open UTG profitably.

questions.prompt: Standard 9-max UTG open sizing in live $1/$2?

answers.explanation: Limping UTG is almost always a leak; this is intentionally wrong.
Deliberately also marked correct so this question has two is_correct=true answers.
Too small for live cash with many players behind.

questions.prompt: You open AKs from UTG and the BB calls. What is the most theoretically sound action on the flop?

answers.explanation: Forfeits value with top pair on a dry board.
Small c-bet maximizes EV on dry boards with strong made hands.
Folds out the hands you want to call.
Overbetting AK on a dry rainbow board is a clear leak.
Folding here is overly tight; included only so the answers array is well-formed.
Marked correct only so this question has exactly one is_correct=true; the deliberate fault is the missing prompt.
Marked wrong only so this question still parses cleanly otherwise.
Marked wrong only so this question still parses cleanly otherwise.
